_comment: References the drafting subagents flagged as genuine student obstacles but could NOT cite from the verified source menu. Next efficient step: add + WebFetch-verify these sources (mostly Britannica articles and a few Project Gutenberg public-domain texts), then author the notes. Each unlocks one or more public notes. Do NOT cite a source until its page is verified. Note: britannica-peter-the-great ALREADY exists verified (use it for the Czar Peter note in chapter-012).

candidates.ref: Isaac Watts, 'There Is a Land of Pure Delight' (1707 hymn)
candidates.source: Project Gutenberg: Watts, Hymns and Spiritual Songs

_comment_66_135: References the Ch 66-135 drafting subagents flagged as real student obstacles but mis-cited (usually to starbuck-history-of-american-whale-fishery or bulfinch) and the lead dropped during vetting. Each needs a real non-whaling, non-mythology source verified by WebFetch before authoring. Battle of Actium: the /event/Battle-of-Actium and /topic/Battle-of-Actium Britannica URLs both 404'd on 2026-06-08; find the live slug first.
